reforms.description: The Third Development Plan for the period 1968-1973 includes substantial tax exemptions and subsidies for investment in the tourism sector, aimed at enhancing private enterprise involvement in tourism development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing private investment in tourism, the reform aims to stimulate economic growth through increased tourist activity and associated spending.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan's targets were revised and implemented, with substantial investments made in the tourism sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in policy towards private sector involvement in tourism, which is crucial for economic development in Portugal.
reforms.source_quote: The action of the authorities will be limited, in principle, to developing essential infrastructures in priority areas and to implementing a policy of incentives in the form of very substantial tax exemptions and subsidies granted fairly liberally for investment purposes.
